articles.title: Oyo raises alarm over heat wave, asks residents to limit outdoor activities - Blueprint Newspapers
articles.search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
The Oyo state government Monday warned residents in the state to limit their outdoor activities between 12:00 and 4:00 p.m when the sun is at its peak.
The state Commissioner for Environment and Natural Resources, Hon. Ademola Aderinto, handed down the warning in a statement made available to journalists in Ibadan.
Hon Aderinto said the warning became necessary with daytime temperatures reaching as high as 41°C, thereby heightened risk of heat exhaustion, dehydration, and heatstroke, particularly during the peak dry season.

articles.title: IMD issues yellow alert for rise in maximum temperatures - The Times of India
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
Kochi:
IMD issued a yellow alert for a 2-3 degree rise in maximum temperatures in state. It said hot and humid weather will prevail in 12 districts until Thursday.
Maximum temperatures are very likely to be around 38°C at isolated places in Kollam and Palakkad, 37°C in Alappuzha, Pathanamthitta, Kottayam, Ernakulam and Thrissur and 36°C in Thiruvananthapuram, Malappuram, Kozhikode, Kannur and Kasargod till Thursday.
According to the temperatures recorded on Monday morning, the maximum temperature was well above normal in Kottayam and Kollam. Punalur recorded the highest maximum temperature of 38.4°C and the lowest minimum temperature of 22.6°C.
IMD said the trough/wind discontinuity from Gulf of Mannar to south Madhya Maharashtra across Tamil Nadu and interior Karnataka runs from Gulf of Mannar to West Vidarbha across Tamil Nadu, interior Karnataka and Marathwada. Upper air cyclonic circulation over south Interior Karnataka and neighbourhood has become less marked.
